title: Centre-Val de Loire

infobox.key: • [President of the Regional Council](/wiki/President_of_the_Regional_Council_(France) "President of the Regional Council (France)")
[NUTS Region](/wiki/First_level_NUTS_of_the_European_Union "First level NUTS of the European Union")

infobox.value: ([/ˌvæl də ˈlwɑːr, ˌvɑːl-/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); French pronunciation: [[sɑ̃tʁ(ə) val də lwaʁ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), lit. 'Centre-Loire Valley'), or simply **Centre** as it was known until 2015, is one of the eighteen [administrative regions](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France") of [France](/wiki/France "France"). It straddles the middle [Loire Valley](/wiki/Loire_Valley "Loire Valley") in the interior of the country, encompassing six departments ([Cher](/wiki/Cher_(department) "Cher (department)"), [Eure-et-Loir](/wiki/Eure-et-Loir "Eure-et-Loir"), [Indre](/wiki/Indre "Indre"), [Indre-et-Loire](/wiki/Indre-et-Loire "Indre-et-Loire"), [Loir-et-Cher](/wiki/Loir-et-Cher "Loir-et-Cher") and [Loiret](/wiki/Loiret "Loiret")), with a population of 2,572,853 as of 2018. Its [prefecture](/wiki/Prefectures_in_France "Prefectures in France") is [Orléans](/wiki/Orl%C3%A9ans "Orléans"), and its largest city is [Tours](/wiki/Tours "Tours").
["Populations de référence 2023"](https://www.insee.fr/fr/statistiques/8681011) (in French). National Institute of Statistics and Economic Studies. 18

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Administrative region of France

This article is about an administrative region of France. For other similarly named regions, see [Central Region (disambiguation)](/wiki/Central_Region_(disambiguation) "Central Region (disambiguation)").

**Centre-Val de Loire** ([/ˌvæl də ˈlwɑːr, ˌvɑːl-/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); French pronunciation: [[sɑ̃tʁ(ə) val də lwaʁ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), lit. 'Centre-Loire Valley'), or simply **Centre** as it was known until 2015, is one of the eighteen [administrative regions](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France") of [France](/wiki/France "France"). It straddles the middle [Loire Valley](/wiki/Loire_Valley "Loire Valley") in the interior of the country, encompassing six departments ([Cher](/wiki/Cher_(department) "Cher (department)"), [Eure-et-Loir](/wiki/Eure-et-Loir "Eure-et-Loir"), [Indre](/wiki/Indre "Indre"), [Indre-et-Loire](/wiki/Indre-et-Loire "Indre-et-Loire"), [Loir-et-Cher](/wiki/Loir-et-Cher "Loir-et-Cher") and [Loiret](/wiki/Loiret "Loiret")), with a population of 2,572,853 as of 2018. Its [prefecture](/wiki/Prefectures_in_France "Prefectures in France") is [Orléans](/wiki/Orl%C3%A9ans "Orléans"), and its largest city is [Tours](/wiki/Tours "Tours").

## Naming and etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Centre-Val_de_Loire&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Naming and etymology")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/3/34/Logo-Centre.svg/120px-Logo-Centre.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Logo-Centre.svg)

Logo used until 2015

Like many current regions of France, the region of Centre-Val de Loire was created from parts of [historical provinces](/wiki/Province_of_France "Province of France"): [Touraine](/wiki/Touraine "Touraine"), [Orléanais](/wiki/Orl%C3%A9anais "Orléanais") and [Berry](/wiki/Berry,_France "Berry, France"). First, the name *Centre* was chosen by the government purely on the basis of geography, in reference to its location in [northwest-central France](/wiki/Geography_of_France "Geography of France") (the central part of the [original French language area](/wiki/Langues_d%27o%C3%AFl "Langues d'oïl")). The name was criticised as being too dull and undistinguished.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Proposed names for the region included *Val de Loire* after the [Loire Valley](/wiki/Loire_Valley "Loire Valley") (a principal feature of the region) or *Cœur de Loire* (Heart of Loire).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] On 17 January 2015, as part of the reorganisation of French regions, the region's official name was changed to *Centre-Val de Loire*.[[3]](#cite_note-3) A new logo was also created for the region.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Centre-Val_de_Loire&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b0/1789_et_2022%2C_Centre-Val_de_Loire_et_%C3%8Ele-de-France%2C_noms.png/250px-1789_et_2022%2C_Centre-Val_de_Loire_et_%C3%8Ele-de-France%2C_noms.png)](/wiki/File:1789_et_2022,_Centre-Val_de_Loire_et_%C3%8Ele-de-France,_noms.png)

The regions Centre-Val de Loire and Île-de-France and their departments on the background of historical provinces

Bordering six out of eighteen, Centre-Val-de-Loire is the region that has the highest number of shared borders with other French regions. The adjacent regions are [Normandy](/wiki/Normandy "Normandy") to the northwest, [Île-de-France](/wiki/%C3%8Ele-de-France "Île-de-France") to the northeast, [Bourgogne-Franche-Comté](/wiki/Bourgogne-Franche-Comt%C3%A9 "Bourgogne-Franche-Comté") to the east, [Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes](/wiki/Auvergne-Rh%C3%B4ne-Alpes "Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes") to the southeast, [Nouvelle-Aquitaine](/wiki/Nouvelle-Aquitaine "Nouvelle-Aquitaine") to the southwest and [Pays de la Loire](/wiki/Pays_de_la_Loire "Pays de la Loire") to the west.

### Departments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Centre-Val_de_Loire&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Departments")]

Centre-Val de Loire comprises six departments: [Cher](/wiki/Cher_(department) "Cher (department)"), [Eure-et-Loir](/wiki/Eure-et-Loir "Eure-et-Loir"), [Indre](/wiki/Indre "Indre"), [Indre-et-Loire](/wiki/Indre-et-Loire "Indre-et-Loire"), [Loir-et-Cher](/wiki/Loir-et-Cher "Loir-et-Cher"), and [Loiret](/wiki/Loiret "Loiret").

### Largest cities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Centre-Val_de_Loire&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Largest cities")]

* [Tours](/wiki/Tours "Tours") with 136,463 inhabitants (2018)
* [Orléans](/wiki/Orl%C3%A9ans "Orléans") with 116,238 inhabitants (2018)
* [Bourges](/wiki/Bourges "Bourges") with 64,668 inhabitants (2018)
* [Blois](/wiki/Blois "Blois") with 45,871 inhabitants (2018)
* [Châteauroux](/wiki/Ch%C3%A2teauroux "Châteauroux") with 43,442 inhabitants (2018)
* [Chartres](/wiki/Chartres "Chartres") with 38,426 inhabitants (2018)
* [Joué-lès-Tours](/wiki/Jou%C3%A9-l%C3%A8s-Tours "Joué-lès-Tours") with 38,250 inhabitants (2018), [Tours Métropole Val de Loire](/wiki/Tours_M%C3%A9tropole_Val_de_Loire "Tours Métropole Val de Loire")
* [Dreux](/wiki/Dreux "Dreux") with 30,664 inhabitants (2018)
* [Vierzon](/wiki/Vierzon "Vierzon") with 25,725 inhabitants (2018)
* [Olivet](/wiki/Olivet,_Loiret "Olivet, Loiret") with 22,168 inhabitants (2018), [Orléans Métropole](/wiki/Orl%C3%A9ans_M%C3%A9tropole "Orléans Métropole")

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Centre-Val_de_Loire&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Economy")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/47/Chambord_Castle_Northwest_facade.jpg/250px-Chambord_Castle_Northwest_facade.jpg)](/wiki/File:Chambord_Castle_Northwest_facade.jpg)

[Château de Chambord](/wiki/Ch%C3%A2teau_de_Chambord "Château de Chambord")

The [gross domestic product](/wiki/Gross_domestic_product "Gross domestic product") (GDP) of the region was 72.4 billion euros in 2018, accounting for 3.1% of French economic output. GDP per capita adjusted for purchasing power was 25,200 euros or 84% of the EU27 average in the same year. The GDP per employee was 99% of the EU average.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

An economic development agency, called *Centréco*, was created in 1994 by the Regional Council of Centre to promote the inflow of investments and the establishment of new businesses by French and foreign companies in the Centre region. This contributes to a mission of economic promotion, international support to regional companies, and the promotion of regional agrofood products via a regional signature, *du Centre*.

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Centre-Val_de_Loire&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Transport")]

[Tours Val de Loire Airport](/wiki/Tours_Val_de_Loire_Airport "Tours Val de Loire Airport") is the only airport in the region that provides limited flights to some destinations. However, the region would normally uses Paris's [Charles de Gaulle Airport](/wiki/Charles_de_Gaulle_Airport "Charles de Gaulle Airport") as it provides more domestic and international destinations. It is accessible by train which takes an hour and 40 minutes from Tours.
